title: Fix blank page / white screen error on app startup before authentication

requirements.summary: Fix the blank white screen that occurs on app startup before the authentication flow begins by ensuring a visible loading state or login page is displayed immediately on launch without crashing.
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the app never results in a blank white screen at any point before authentication.
A loading spinner or the login page is displayed immediately on app launch.
No unhandled JavaScript errors are thrown during the initialization phase before the authentication check completes.
The ErrorBoundary in App.tsx catches any remaining initialization errors and displays a readable fallback UI instead of a blank screen.
The app correctly reaches the LoginPage or an authenticated route after initialization completes without requiring a manual reload.

file_operations.description: Add an immediate loading state display before any Internet Identity initialization or routing logic runs. Wrap the entire router and query provider setup in a conditional render that shows a centered loading spinner while the InternetIdentityProvider initializes. Ensure the ErrorBoundary wrapper correctly catches and displays initialization errors instead of leaving a blank screen. Add a fallback loading UI that appears synchronously before any async authentication checks.

Ensure the AuthenticatedOnly guard displays a loading spinner while the Internet Identity state (identity and loginStatus) is still initializing. Add an explicit check for loginStatus === 'initializing' or 'logging-in' before attempting to read the identity or redirect. Prevent any child components from rendering until the authentication state is fully resolved. Ensure the loading spinner is visible and centered during the entire initialization phase.

requirements.summary: Audit and fix the initialization sequence in App.tsx and the AuthenticatedOnly guard to ensure the Internet Identity authentication state is fully initialized before any route rendering or data fetching is attempted, preventing crashes from accessing undefined identity state.
acceptanceCriteria: The AuthenticatedOnly guard shows a loading spinner while authentication state is still initializing.
No component attempts to use the actor or identity before the AuthClient has finished its initialization.
The useActor hook does not throw or return an invalid actor during the initialization phase.
The QueryClientProvider and router are initialized in an order that prevents queries from firing before the identity is ready.

file_operations.description: Audit the initialization order of QueryClientProvider, InternetIdentityProvider, and the router. Ensure that the InternetIdentityProvider has fully initialized (loginStatus is not 'initializing') before rendering the router or any child components that might trigger queries. Add a synchronous loading state at the top level of App.tsx that prevents rendering any children until the identity provider has completed initialization. Coordinate with the ErrorBoundary to ensure any initialization errors are caught and displayed instead of crashing silently.

Add explicit guards to check if loginStatus is 'initializing' or 'logging-in' before attempting to read identity or redirect. Display a loading spinner during these states and prevent rendering of children or navigation until the identity is confirmed ready. Ensure no queries or actor calls can fire from child components while the guard is in a loading state.

Enhance the ErrorBoundary to catch initialization-phase errors specifically. Add logging to identify when errors occur during the authentication initialization sequence. Ensure the fallback UI is always visible and never results in a blank screen. Consider adding a specific message or recovery action for authentication initialization failures.
